noteP1: We picked fifteen companies from our portfolio that map to the priorities of Kohl's's technology organization: frontier AI and the infrastructure to run it economically, the data foundations underneath, and the security and developer tooling that let both move to production.
noteP2: Each card opens to a brief and the angle we think fits Kohl's best, and you can filter by category. Our full portfolio of 150+ active companies is linked in the top right. Whenever it's convenient, send back the ones worth a closer look.
portfolioIntro: Every company here works at the infrastructure layer, across AI, data, security, and developer tooling, with an angle on where it fits Kohl's's agenda. Filter by category, then expand any card for the brief.

anthropic.pull: Kohl's is rebuilding foundational data architecture and website structure to support better search, availability, personalization and an AI-ready digital ecosystem. That makes the model layer consequential: it must reason over customer profiles, product attributes, search behavior, promotions, loyalty activity and store inventory while remaining governable enough for turnaround economics, privacy, payment security, availability and legacy dependencies. Anthropic matters because Claude combines long-context reasoning, agent capabilities and Claude Code with an explicit emphasis on steerability and predictable enterprise behavior. It could help merchants, marketers, engineers, store teams, planners and customer-service staff work through complex material, give engineering teams a common assistant across commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications, and support agents without turning the model into the final authority. The fit is strongest where Kohl's needs higher digital relevance and inventory visibility without adding operational complexity.
cases: Reason across customer profiles, product attributes, search behavior, promotions, loyalty activity and store inventory without forcing teams into a separate knowledge system.
Use Claude Code across commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications and the integration services behind them.
Keep human review, model policy and audit evidence explicit in high-consequence workflows.

wispr.pull: For Kohl's, the fastest route to better operational data may be improving how people enter it. Wispr Flow sits beneath existing software as a voice-first input layer, allowing merchants, marketers, engineers, store teams, planners and customer-service staff to dictate store observations, customer calls, merchandising notes, vendor discussions and service interactions into the systems already in use. Spoken observations become usable text at the speed of the work, including on mobile. That would reduce documentation friction across commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications, preserve more of the rationale behind decisions and create a higher-quality record for analytics, handoffs and agentic workflows.
cases: Turn store observations, customer calls, merchandising notes, vendor discussions and service interactions into structured entries while the context is still fresh.
Work inside commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications rather than creating another application to manage.
Create a richer decision trail for handoffs, analytics and supervised agents.

browserbase.pull: A meaningful share of Kohl's work still crosses vendor sites, marketplace consoles, store tools, carrier portals and legacy retail systems. Those browser-bound steps are hard to automate reliably because they carry session state, changing interfaces and sensitive credentials. Browserbase matters as secure, programmable browser infrastructure for agents: it can let the technology organization automate web workflows and test commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications at scale without building a brittle fleet of headless browsers. The fit is strongest for repeatable, supervised tasks inside search-to-cart, promotion, fulfillment and store-service workflows, where centralized sessions, logs and controls are as important as the automation itself.
cases: Automate bounded tasks across vendor sites, marketplace consoles, store tools, carrier portals and legacy retail systems without waiting for every system to expose an API.
Run repeatable browser tests across commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications before customer- or employee-facing releases.
Centralize sessions, credentials and execution logs for governed agent activity.

coder.pull: Kohl's engineering surface spans commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications, with different dependencies, access requirements and release cadences. Coder gives the technology organization a way to define those environments once and deliver them as secure cloud workspaces. Developers gain a ready-to-use stack; security teams keep code and sensitive tooling away from unmanaged endpoints; platform teams can observe utilization and control compute. That creates a practical foundation for higher digital relevance and inventory visibility without adding operational complexity, particularly as AI-assisted development increases both the pace of change and the need for reproducible environments.
cases: Standardize cloud workspaces for teams building commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications.
Keep source code, credentials and approved AI tooling inside controlled infrastructure.
Reduce onboarding and environment drift across distributed, acquired or specialized teams.

token.pull: As Kohl's adds agents to search-to-cart, promotion, fulfillment and store-service workflows, every useful workflow introduces a non-human principal with credentials, permissions and an owner—or an ownership gap. Token Security matters because it treats those identities as a distinct governance domain rather than an extension of employee access. The platform can continuously find commerce services, promotion engines, integration accounts, bots and AI agents, govern their lifecycle and enforce access based on intended purpose. That would help Kohl's scale automation while keeping the blast radius around customer, product, search, promotion, loyalty, inventory and store data narrow and the audit trail clear.
cases: Inventory commerce services, promotion engines, integration accounts, bots and AI agents and tie every credential to an accountable owner.
Constrain agent access to the specific customer, product, search, promotion, loyalty, inventory and store data required for each workflow.
Preserve lifecycle and entitlement evidence for audits, regulators and internal risk review.

fal.pull: Kohl's has practical multimodal needs across product imagery, retail-media creative, beauty content, training video and personalized offers. fal matters because it gives product and engineering teams a high-speed inference layer for image, video, audio and voice models through simple APIs, without forcing each use case to become an infrastructure project. The technology organization could use it to prototype and scale bounded generative experiences alongside commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications, from content production to simulation or visual assistance. The strongest fit is where latency and iteration speed matter but the risk profile around turnaround economics, privacy, payment security, availability and legacy dependencies still requires approved models, controlled inputs and a clear review path.
cases: Embed image, video, audio or voice generation into new search, personalization, retail-media, inventory and store-service capabilities.
Prototype against multiple media models through one developer-facing inference layer.
Keep review, consent and brand or safety controls in the surrounding application workflow.

montecarlo.pull: Kohl's AI and operating decisions increasingly depend on customer, product, search, promotion, loyalty, inventory and store data. A broken pipeline, silent schema change or weak model input can therefore surface as a service, safety or operational problem rather than a data-team ticket. Monte Carlo matters because it unifies observability across data pipelines, systems, code, models and the agents built on top. The technology organization would gain a common view of lineage, incidents and downstream impact across search, personalization, inventory, marketing and store-support agents. That trust layer is central to higher digital relevance and inventory visibility without adding operational complexity, especially where early detection and clear ownership are essential to managing turnaround economics, privacy, payment security, availability and legacy dependencies.
cases: Monitor customer, product, search, promotion, loyalty, inventory and store data from source through dashboards, models and agent actions.
Trace incidents to the affected commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications and accountable engineering teams.
Prevent silent data failures from undermining higher digital relevance and inventory visibility without adding operational complexity.

tonic.pull: For Kohl's, better test data can improve both delivery speed and production safety. Tonic can create high-fidelity synthetic versions of customer, loyalty, payment, product, promotion and inventory data, allowing developers to exercise commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications against realistic volumes and relationships without relying on raw production data. The same approach can support model training, fine-tuning and evaluation for search, personalization, inventory, marketing and store-support agents. The strategic fit is strongest where data provisioning is slowed by turnaround economics, privacy, payment security, availability and legacy dependencies, yet inadequate test coverage would expose the organization to even greater operational or customer risk.
cases: Replace production copies of customer, loyalty, payment, product, promotion and inventory data in development and test environments.
Generate rare and edge-case scenarios for commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications and AI evaluation.
Reduce privacy exposure while giving teams faster access to useful data.

netbox.pull: Kohl's depends on stores, distribution centers, cloud services, corporate networks and partner connections, but automation is only as reliable as the inventory and configuration data behind it. NetBox Labs matters as a source of truth for documenting, managing and automating IT and network infrastructure. It could give the technology organization one current model of sites, devices, circuits, addresses and dependencies across that estate. That foundation makes changes to hybrid retail and cloud workloads that must be modernized while the business protects cost and uptime safer, improves incident context and gives infrastructure agents a trusted state to read before they act. It replaces stale spreadsheets with governed infrastructure context and supports higher digital relevance and inventory visibility without adding operational complexity.
cases: Create a maintained source of truth across stores, distribution centers, cloud services, corporate networks and partner connections.
Drive provisioning and change validation for hybrid retail and cloud workloads that must be modernized while the business protects cost and uptime from intended state.
Ground infrastructure automation and agents in accurate topology and ownership data.

valar.pull: For Kohl's, the highest-value AI workloads sit close to customer, product, search, promotion, loyalty, inventory and store data and often need bursty batch or agentic execution. Valar provides an in-VPC inference platform built for that pattern, using scheduling, routing and accelerator placement to turn installed compute into more usable capacity. It would give platform teams one operating layer for search, personalization, inventory, marketing and store-support agents, while prompts, outputs and telemetry remain within the enterprise boundary. That makes Valar a strong fit where external model traffic is constrained by turnaround economics, privacy, payment security, availability and legacy dependencies, but the business still needs production-grade throughput.
cases: Run search, personalization, inventory, marketing and store-support agents inside Kohl's own VPC and security boundary.
Increase usable accelerator capacity through scheduling, batching and routing.
Keep prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies under enterprise control.

echo.pull: Software supply-chain risk at Kohl's begins with what applications and agents are built on. Echo provides CVE-free, maintained alternatives for container bases, libraries, VMs, Helm charts and end-of-life components, removing known vulnerability work at the source. Applied to commerce, search, loyalty, inventory, store and data-platform applications, it would let engineering and security teams spend less time patching the same underlying package across many products. That supports higher digital relevance and inventory visibility without adding operational complexity while strengthening the evidence around consumer privacy, payments, advertising, accessibility, third-party risk and cost controls, particularly for workloads that need hardened or FIPS-aligned components.
cases: Standardize hardened dependencies across commerce, search, loyalty, inventory, store and data-platform applications.
Remove known vulnerabilities before they enter application and agent build pipelines.
Use FIPS-validated options where stronger assurance is required.

act.pull: Kohl's cloud estate now includes hybrid retail and cloud workloads that must be modernized while the business protects cost and uptime, with services and agents communicating across boundaries that are difficult to understand from identity or network controls alone. Act Security matters because it learns how applications are intended to communicate, compares that with actual behavior and guides teams in closing unnecessary paths. The agentless approach could help the technology organization create business-context perimeters around commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications without redesigning the network first. That is a direct fit for turnaround economics, privacy, payment security, availability and legacy dependencies, where one over-permissioned pathway can expand the blast radius of a compromise.
cases: Map intended and actual communication across hybrid retail and cloud workloads that must be modernized while the business protects cost and uptime.
Create least-privilege perimeters around commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications and their data services.
Reduce lateral-movement risk without deploying another host agent.

clover.pull: For Kohl's, shipping faster only works if security can understand the roadmap at the same speed. Clover Security integrates into the design and planning stage, where merchants, marketers, engineers, store teams, planners and customer-service staff are defining new search, personalization, retail-media, inventory and store-service capabilities. It can surface risks from the context already captured in tickets and documents, then return specific requirements before architecture and code harden around a weak assumption. The fit is strongest for commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications, where early decisions touch turnaround economics, privacy, payment security, availability and legacy dependencies and where a preventable redesign near release would slow the broader technology program.
cases: Review new search, personalization, retail-media, inventory and store-service capabilities while requirements and architecture are still being shaped.
Map security decisions back to tickets, design documents and accountable owners.
Reduce late-stage rework across commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications without adding another approval queue.

drata.pull: The assurance burden at Kohl's spans consumer privacy, payments, advertising, accessibility, third-party risk and cost controls, with evidence distributed across cloud platforms, business systems and external partners. Drata provides one continuous layer for interpreting those signals, assigning ownership and maintaining audit readiness. It can reduce repetitive evidence work around hybrid retail and cloud workloads that must be modernized while the business protects cost and uptime while giving risk and technology leaders a shared view of control health. The value is not simply a faster audit; it is earlier visibility into where policy, system behavior or third-party posture has diverged from the standard required for search-to-cart, promotion, fulfillment and store-service workflows.
cases: Continuously map controls and evidence across commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications and hybrid retail and cloud workloads that must be modernized while the business protects cost and uptime.
Reuse assurance work across consumer privacy, payments, advertising, accessibility, third-party risk and cost controls instead of rebuilding it for every review.
Give leaders a current view of control drift, ownership and third-party risk.

orca.pull: Kohl's is a large omnichannel retailer managing a turnaround across more than 1,100 stores, digital channels, loyalty and a major beauty partnership. That scale creates a cloud-security requirement for broad coverage with low operational impact. Orca Security provides that through an agentless CNAPP spanning workload protection, data security, posture, vulnerability management and compliance. The platform can identify dangerous combinations around commerce, search, loyalty, promotion, inventory, store and retail-media applications, including exposed data, over-privileged identities and reachable vulnerabilities, then route teams toward the highest-consequence fixes. For an organization managing turnaround economics, privacy, payment security, availability and legacy dependencies, that context-aware prioritization is more useful than adding another feed of undifferentiated alerts.
cases: Gain agentless visibility across Kohl's cloud estate and emerging AI workloads.
Prioritize attack paths into sensitive Kohl's data and critical applications.
Unify posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance remediation in one view.
